title: Intriguingly Spiced Middle Eastern Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and aromatic Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb, rolled oats, and a blend of ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly spices, with a hint of mustard and a bouncy texture, perfect for a festive occasion.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, yellow mustard, garam masala, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Let the lamb marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Sprinkle the rolled oats over the lamb cubes, making sure they stick to the meat.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the lamb is cooked through and the oats are lightly toasted.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Serve with a side of quinoa or couscous and your favorite steamed vegetables.
Let the kebabs rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
